name: Uncle Shels Badlands Bushwaker Sauce
recipeIngredient: 3 tbsp Jack Daniels or other bourbon
2 tsp Juice of lemon or lime
2 tsp Hot or sweet Hungarian Paprika
recipeInstructions:
1. Saute the onion in the oil over medium-high heat until translucent.
2. Add the minced garlic and reduce heat to medium. Continue cooking for another minute or two.
3. Add the tomato sauce and stir well to mix.
4. Add the remaining ingredients, one at a time (down to and including honey), stirring after each addition.
5. Grind all the spice blend ingredients until you get a medium fine, somewhat grainy result.
6. Add the habanero and paprika and blend together.
7. Stir in the spice mixture to the sauce, 1 teaspoon at a time, until you find the desired heat/flavor level (about 4-5 averages).
8. After a minute or so, add the bourbon and lemon juice. Let the sauce heat for another five minutes or so, stirring occasionally.
9. Turn off the heat. Let the sauce steep in the fridge for at least half an hour or more to let flavors meld.
10. Taste and adjust your spice mixture as needed.
11. Serve as a hot table sauce. This is not a marinade or cooking sauce, although you might add a light brushing to meat or chicken about 15 minutes before removing from the grill.
Orange-Habanero-Chipotle oil:
3-4 Chipotle dried peppers
1 Dried habanero
2-3 tbsp Dried orange peel (minced)
2 cups Canola oil
Combine dry ingredients in a spice mill and grind fine. Mix with canola oil in a clean glass jar with a non-reactive top. Let the mixture sit in a cool place away from sunlight for 2-3 days, shaking the jar 2-3 times per day. Filter the oil through a paper coffee filter into a clean glass jar. Store in the fridge up to 6 months. Note: It may take some time for the oil to run through the filter completely.
